1.template: And I know that the record which I make is true; and I make it with mine own hand; and I make it {0} to my knowledge.

1.title: A book from heaven
1.text: In the vision, Lehi is given a book and reads it; written in it are the destruction of Jerusalem and the coming of a Messiah to redeem mankind.

1.question: In Lehi’s vision, the brightness of the One descending from heaven was above that of what?

1.template: And he read, saying: Wo, wo, unto Jerusalem, for I have seen thine abominations! Yea, and many things did my father read concerning Jerusalem —that it should be destroyed, and the inhabitants thereof; many should perish by the sword, and many should be carried away captive into {0}.
the tender {0} of the Lord are over all those whom he hath chosen

2.template: And it came to pass that he was {0} unto the word of the Lord, wherefore he did as the Lord commanded him.

2.title: Leaving everything behind
2.text: Lehi obeyed at once: he departed into the wilderness and left his house, his inheritance, his gold, his silver, and his precious things, taking only his family and provisions.

2.template: And it came to pass that he called the name of the river, {0}, and it emptied into the Red Sea; and the valley was in the borders near the mouth thereof.

2.question: Which two of Lehi’s sons murmured against him for leaving Jerusalem?

2.template: ye shall prosper, and shall be led to a land of {0}
And inasmuch as thou shalt keep my {0}, thou shalt be made a ruler and a teacher over thy brethren.

3.title: The brass plates
3.text: The brass plates held the record of the Jews and the genealogy of Lehi's forefathers, engraven on metal. Nephi's people would keep and add to such records for a thousand years.

3.question: What did the Lord command Lehi's sons to obtain from Laban?
options: The records engraven on the plates of brass
A ship to cross the sea

3.template: I will go and do the things which the Lord hath {0}

3.question: When they cast lots, upon whom did the lot fall to go in unto Laban first?

3.template: that we may preserve unto our children the {0} of our fathers

3.question: Why did Laban thrust the brothers out and send servants to slay them?
options: He lusted after their gold, silver, and precious things
He wished to keep the city safe

3.question: Where did Nephi and his brothers hide after fleeing the servants of Laban?
options: In the cavity of a rock

3.title: An angel intervenes
3.text: As Laman and Lemuel beat their younger brothers with a rod, an angel of the Lord appeared and rebuked them, declaring that Nephi had been chosen to be a ruler over them, and that the Lord would deliver Laban into their hands.

3.question: Doubting again, how many men did Laman and Lemuel say Laban could command?

4.title: Be strong like Moses
4.text: To give his brothers courage, Nephi recalls the Exodus: Moses spoke to the Red Sea and it divided, their fathers crossed on dry ground, and Pharaoh's armies were drowned. If God could do that, surely he could deliver them from Laban.

4.question: Which prophet's example did Nephi use to urge his brothers to be strong?

4.template: And I was led by the {0}, not knowing beforehand the things which I should do.
It is better that one man should perish than that a {0} should dwindle and perish in unbelief.

4.question: Which item of Laban's did Nephi take for himself?
Why did Laban's servant suppose Nephi to be his master Laban?
options: Nephi wore Laban's garments and sword
It was too dark to see

4.template: when Laman saw me he was exceedingly {0}

pairs.right: Keeper of the brass plates, slain by Nephi
Servant of Laban who swore to join Lehi's family
The son who obtained the records
Eldest brother who murmured and doubted

4.question: Besides Zoram, what did they take with them into the wilderness to their father's tent?

5.title: Sariah, a named matriarch
5.text: Sariah, Lehi's wife, is one of the few women named in the Book of Mormon. Fearing her sons had perished, she had mourned and complained; their safe return turned her grief into faith and rejoicing.

5.template: I know that I am a {0} man

5.question: After the sons returned safely, what did the family offer to the Lord in thanksgiving?

5.template: they did contain the five books of {0}

5.title: Descendants of Joseph
5.text: The genealogy revealed that Lehi descended from Joseph — the son of Jacob who was sold into Egypt and later preserved his family from famine. Even Laban, keeper of the plates, was of the same lineage.

5.template: these plates of brass should never {0}

5.question: Why were the records of such great worth to Nephi's family?
options: So they could preserve the commandments of the Lord unto their children
Because they were made of gold
Because they showed the way back to Jerusalem

6.title: Why Nephi omits the genealogy
6.text: Nephi deliberately leaves the full genealogy on his father's record, since the space on his own plates is precious. He keeps only what is of worth: it is enough, he says, to know that they are descendants of Joseph.

6.template: and the God of Jacob, and be {0}

6.question: What does Nephi command his seed concerning these plates?
options: Not to fill them with things of no worth to the children of men
To bury them in the wilderness
To give them to the Jews

7.title: Ishmael's family
7.text: Lehi sends his sons back for Ishmael's household so they can marry. Ishmael's sons and daughters would become ancestors of the Lamanites and Nephites alongside Lehi's children.

7.question: Whom did Nephi and his brethren return to Jerusalem to bring into the wilderness?
Who joined Laman and Lemuel in rebelling during the journey?
options: Two daughters and the two sons of Ishmael and their families

7.template: in delivering us out of the hands of {0}

7.question: How did his brothers respond when Nephi rebuked them?
options: They bound him with cords and sought his life
They wept and repented at once

7.title: Bands loosed by faith
7.text: When Nephi prayed for strength according to his faith, the cords binding his hands and feet fell away. He repeatedly turned to prayer in moments of danger throughout his record.

7.question: What did the families do when they reached Lehi's tent?
options: Gave thanks and offered sacrifice and burnt offerings

8.title: The tree of life vision
8.text: Lehi's dream is the Book of Mormon's most famous vision. Each element - the tree, the iron rod, the path, the mist, and the building - carries meaning that Nephi will later explain.

8.question: Who came and stood before Lehi in his dream, bidding him follow?
options: A man dressed in a white robe

8.question: What did Lehi behold whose fruit was desirable to make one happy?

8.template: I beheld that the fruit thereof was {0}

8.question: What did Lehi behold extending along the bank of the river, leading to the tree?
What arose that caused those on the path to lose their way?
What were the people in the great and spacious building doing?
options: Mocking and pointing fingers at those partaking of the fruit
Holding to the rod of iron

8.title: Holding to the rod
8.text: Lehi saw that only those who held continually to the iron rod reached the tree and its fruit. Others let go and were lost in the mist or wandered toward the great and spacious building.

pairs.right: Led to the tree of life
Caused many to lose their way

8.question: In Lehi's dream, who partook not of the fruit?

8.template: to keep the {0} of the Lord

9.title: Two sets of plates
9.text: Nephi kept two records: the small plates (these), devoted to sacred and prophetic matters, and the large plates, devoted to the reign of the kings, the wars, and the history of his people.

9.question: What did Nephi say should be engraven on the 'other plates'?
options: The reign of the kings, and the wars and contentions of his people
The ministry and teachings of his people
Lehi's dream of the tree of life

9.question: Why did Nephi say the Lord commanded him to make these plates?
options: For a wise purpose in the Lord, which Nephi did not know
To record the wars of his people

9.template: he hath all {0} unto the fulfilling of all his words

10.question: What did Lehi prophesy concerning the Jews after Jerusalem's destruction?
options: They would be carried captive to Babylon and later return to their land
They would never again possess their land

10.template: from the time that my father left {0}

10.title: A prophet to prepare the way
10.text: About 600 years beforehand, Lehi foresaw John the Baptist, who would cry in the wilderness, baptize with water, and baptize the Messiah himself.

10.question: What did Lehi say the prophet would cry in the wilderness?
options: Prepare ye the way of the Lord, and make his paths straight
Repent, for the kingdom is at hand
Come and see the Lamb of God

10.question: After the Messiah would be slain, what did Lehi prophesy he would do?
options: Rise from the dead and manifest himself to the Gentiles by the Holy Ghost

pairs.right: Takes away the sins of the world
Symbol of the house of Israel

10.title: Nephi seeks to see for himself
10.text: Hearing his father's vision, Nephi desired to see and know the same things by the power of the Holy Ghost - the gift of God to all who diligently seek him. His own vision follows in the next chapters.

10.question: What did Nephi teach about those who diligently seek the Lord?
options: He that diligently seeketh shall find, and the mysteries of God shall be unfolded unto them
Only prophets can know the mysteries of God
The Lord changes with each generation
The way is open only to the house of Israel

10.question: What did Nephi teach about the unclean and the judgment-seat of God?
options: No unclean thing can dwell with God, so the unclean must be cast off
The unclean are cleansed automatically at judgment
God dwells with all people regardless
Only the Jews face the judgment-seat

11.title: Caught up into a mountain
11.text: Pondering his father's words, Nephi was caught away by the Spirit to an exceedingly high mountain he had never seen. There he was shown the same vision of the tree of life, with its full interpretation.

11.question: When Nephi desired to see his father's vision, what did the Spirit ask if he believed his father had seen?
options: The tree of which his father had spoken

11.question: What sign was given Nephi - whom would he behold descending out of heaven?
options: A man, the Son of God

11.question: In what city did Nephi behold a virgin, exceedingly fair and white?
What did the angel ask Nephi if he understood?

11.title: The mother of the Son of God
11.text: The angel showed Nephi that the virgin of Nazareth was the mother of the Son of God after the manner of the flesh - answering the question about the 'condescension of God,' that God Himself would come down to dwell among men.

11.question: As the virgin bore a child in her arms, what did the angel declare?
options: Behold the Lamb of God, the Son of the Eternal Father
Behold the prophet of the Lord
Behold the king of the Jews

11.template: it is the {0} of God, which sheddeth itself abroad

11.question: In Nephi's vision, who baptized the Lamb of God?
options: The prophet who should prepare the way before him

11.question: What did Nephi see happen to the Lamb of God?
options: He was lifted up upon the cross and slain for the sins of the world
He ascended into heaven in glory
He was hidden away by his apostles

pairs.right: Mother of the Son of God

12.question: What did Nephi see among his people through many generations?
options: Wars, rumors of wars, and great slaughters with the sword

12.title: Signs of destruction
12.text: Nephi foresaw earthquakes, fire, and a vapor of darkness that would sink and burn whole cities in the promised land - the very signs fulfilled at the Savior's death, recorded in 3 Nephi.

12.question: After the destruction, whom did Nephi see descend from heaven and show himself to the survivors?
options: The prophet who prepares the way

12.title: Two sets of twelve
12.text: Nephi saw twelve disciples chosen to minister to his seed in the promised land, distinct from the twelve apostles at Jerusalem. The angel explained that the apostles would judge the house of Israel, and the twelve ministers would judge Nephi's seed.

12.template: their garments are made white in his {0}

12.question: What did Nephi behold pass away in righteousness, their garments white like the Lamb of God?
options: All the nations of the earth

pairs.right: The pride of the children of men
The justice of the Eternal God

12.question: Why did Nephi see the seed of his brethren overpower his own seed?
options: Because of the pride of his seed and the temptations of the devil
Because his seed had no weapons
Because the Lord had abandoned them
Because of famine in the land

12.question: What did the angel say would become of the seed of the brethren?
options: They would build a great temple

13.question: What did the angel call the church Nephi saw form among the Gentiles?
options: Most abominable above all other churches
The church of the Lamb of God

13.title: Founded by the devil
13.text: Nephi saw that the great and abominable church was founded by the devil himself. The angel later called it 'the mother of harlots' and 'the mother of abominations' - it stands for all that fights against the Lamb of God.

13.question: What did the angel say the gold, silver, silks, and harlots represented?
options: The desires of the great and abominable church

13.question: What did the angel say was upon the seed of Nephi's brethren?

13.title: A man across the waters
13.text: Nephi saw the Spirit of God move upon a man among the Gentiles who crossed the many waters to the promised land. Latter-day readers commonly understand this man to be Christopher Columbus.

13.question: As the Gentiles prospered in the land, what did Nephi behold carried forth among them?
What did the angel say the book proceeding from the mouth of a Jew was?
options: A record of the Jews containing the covenants of the Lord
A history of the Gentile kings

13.template: blind the eyes and {0} the hearts of the children of men

13.question: According to the angel, what happened to the book after it passed through the great and abominable church?
options: Many plain and precious things were taken away from it
It was hidden in the temple

13.question: What did the Lamb of God promise concerning the Gentiles?
options: I will be merciful unto the Gentiles
I will destroy the Gentiles utterly
The Gentiles shall never receive my gospel

13.title: The Book of Mormon foreseen
13.text: The Lamb promised that the things written by Nephi's seed would be hid up and come forth to the Gentiles 'by the gift and power of the Lamb' - a prophecy of the Book of Mormon, restoring plain and precious truths.

13.template: how beautiful upon the {0} shall they be

13.question: What did the angel say the 'other books' coming forth would do?
options: Establish the truth of the first records and restore the plain and precious things
Replace the records of the apostles
Reveal the genealogy of the Gentiles
Record the wars of the Nephites

13.question: How did the angel say the Lord would manifest himself to the Jews and the Gentiles?
options: To both, so that the last shall be first and the first shall be last
To neither until the end of the world

14.question: What did the angel say of the Gentiles who hearken to the Lamb and harden not their hearts?
options: They shall be numbered among the house of Israel and be a blessed people
They shall be cast into captivity
They shall lose the promised land

14.question: How many churches did the angel say there are?
options: Only two: the church of the Lamb of God and the church of the devil

14.question: Why were the numbers of the church of the Lamb of God few?
options: Because of the wickedness and abominations of the great whore
Because they lived only in one land

14.template: they were armed with {0} and with the power of God

14.title: The work of the Father
14.text: The angel foretold that when God's wrath is poured out on the mother of harlots, 'the work of the Father shall commence' - preparing the way to fulfill his covenants with the house of Israel.

14.question: When the angel said 'Behold one of the twelve apostles of the Lamb,' what was the man wearing?
Besides the remainder of Nephi's vision, what would this apostle also write about?

14.title: The apostle was John
14.text: The man in the white robe, ordained to write the remainder of the vision and the end of the world, was named John by the angel - the apostle John, author of the book of Revelation.

pairs.left: Church of the Lamb of God
The whore of all the earth

pairs.right: Writes the remainder of the vision

14.question: Why did Nephi write only a small part of the things he saw?
options: He was forbidden; the Lord ordained the apostle John to write the rest
He forgot most of the vision
The angel told him to write everything

15.question: What were Nephi's brethren doing when he returned to the tent?
options: Disputing about the things their father had spoken

15.question: What had the brethren failed to do to understand their father's words?

15.title: Ask, and it shall be made known
15.text: Nephi reminded his brothers of the Lord's promise: if they would not harden their hearts, but ask in faith, believing they would receive, and keep the commandments with diligence, these things would be made known to them.

15.question: To what was the house of Israel compared?
Whose words did Nephi rehearse concerning the restoration of the Jews?
What did Nephi say the tree his father saw represented?
What did Nephi say the river of water his father saw was?
What did Nephi teach about the kingdom of God?
options: No unclean thing can enter into it
It is filthy like the world

15.question: How did Nephi describe the fruit of the tree of life?
options: The greatest of all the gifts of God

16.question: Why, according to Nephi, does the guilty take the truth to be hard?
options: Because it cuts them to the very center
Because it is spoken in anger
Because it is written in a strange language

16.question: Whom did Nephi, his brethren, and Zoram take to wife?

16.title: The Liahona
16.text: At the tent door Lehi found a round ball of fine brass with two spindles that pointed the way through the wilderness. Centuries later the prophet Alma called it the Liahona, meaning 'compass.'

16.question: What did they name the place where they pitched their tents after four days?
After breaking his steel bow, what did Nephi do?
options: Made a bow out of wood and an arrow from a straight stick
Returned to Jerusalem for a new bow

16.title: Guided by faith
16.text: Nephi saw that the pointers in the ball worked according to the family's faith, diligence, and heed, and that a new writing appeared on it from time to time, giving them understanding concerning the ways of the Lord.

16.question: What did Nephi learn from how the ball worked?
options: By small means the Lord can bring about great things
The ball worked only at night
Only Lehi could read the ball
The ball led them back to Jerusalem

16.question: What did Laman propose to Lemuel and the sons of Ishmael?

17.question: How long did the family sojourn in the wilderness?

17.title: Bountiful and Irreantum
17.text: After eight years the family reached a fruitful land by the sea, which they named Bountiful. They called the sea Irreantum, which Nephi says means 'many waters.'

17.question: What did the Lord command Nephi to build?
options: A ship to carry his people across the waters

17.template: I will also be your {0} in the wilderness

17.question: How did the brothers react when Nephi began to build a ship?
options: They murmured and called him a fool
They asked to learn the craft

17.question: What example did Nephi use to answer his murmuring brothers?
options: The children of Israel led out of Egypt

17.question: By Moses' word, what happened to the waters of the Red Sea?
options: They were divided and Israel passed on dry ground
They rose into a great storm

17.question: What did Nephi teach about how the Lord esteems all flesh?
options: He esteemeth all flesh in one; the righteous is favored of God
He favors only the house of Israel
He esteems the rich above the poor
He has rejected all the Gentiles

17.question: With which fathers did Nephi say the Lord made his covenant?

17.template: He sent fiery flying {0} among them

17.title: A still small voice
17.text: Nephi told his brothers that the Lord had spoken to them in a still small voice, but they were past feeling and could not feel his words; so he spoke to them like the voice of thunder.

17.question: When the brethren came to throw Nephi into the sea, what did he do?
options: Filled with the power of God, he forbade them to touch him
He leaped into the sea himself
He called down fire from heaven

17.question: After the Lord shook the brethren, what did they try to do to Nephi?
options: Worship him, but he would not allow it

18.question: How did Nephi build the ship?
options: After the manner the Lord showed him, not after the manner of men
Copying the ships of the Egyptians
With the help of skilled shipbuilders

18.title: Jacob and Joseph
18.text: Two more sons were born to Lehi and Sariah during the eight years in the wilderness: Jacob and Joseph. Jacob would later become a prophet and write his own book in the Book of Mormon.

18.question: How did the brethren and the sons of Ishmael behave on the voyage?
options: They made merry, danced, and spoke with much rudeness, forgetting the Lord
They prayed and gave thanks continually

18.question: What did Laman and Lemuel do to Nephi on the ship?
options: Bound him with cords and treated him harshly

18.template: the {0}, which had been prepared of the Lord, did cease to work

18.title: Lehi and Sariah brought low
18.text: The harshness of Laman and Lemuel during the storm brought the aged Lehi and Sariah near to death upon their sick-beds; even the tears of Nephi's wife and children could not soften his brothers.

18.question: What finally softened the brothers' hearts to loose Nephi?
options: The power of God, when they were about to be swallowed up in the sea

18.question: After the brothers loosed Nephi and he prayed, what happened?
options: The winds and the storm ceased, and there was a great calm

18.question: What did they find on the land of promise?
options: Beasts of every kind and ore of gold, silver, and copper
A people who spoke their language

19.question: What was Nephi commanded to write upon these plates?
options: The ministry and the prophecies, the more plain and precious parts
The wars and contentions of his people

19.question: How long after Lehi left Jerusalem did the angel say the Messiah would come?

pairs.right: Christ would be buried; three days of darkness

19.question: According to the prophet, why would those at Jerusalem be scourged by all people?
options: Because they crucify the God of Israel
Because they kept the law of Moses

19.title: A hiss and a byword
19.text: The prophet foretold that the Jews, for turning their hearts from the Holy One of Israel, would wander, be hated, and become 'a hiss and a byword' among all nations - a prophecy of the long scattering of the Jews.

19.question: What did the Lord promise to do for the house of Israel in the last days?
options: Gather them in from the four quarters of the earth
Make them rulers over the Gentiles

19.template: that it might be for our {0} and learning

19.title: Liken the scriptures
19.text: Nephi read to his brothers from the books of Moses and from Isaiah, and said he 'did liken all scriptures' unto his people - reading them as if written for their own profit and learning.

19.question: How did Nephi address his people as he taught them the words of the prophets?
options: As a remnant of the house of Israel, a branch broken off
As the children of the Gentiles

20.title: Isaiah, quoted in the New World
20.text: First Nephi 20 is Isaiah chapter 48. Nephi read it from the brass plates; the Book of Mormon version adds the phrase 'or out of the waters of baptism,' a clarification not found in the Bible.

20.question: Why did the Lord declare these things before they came to pass?
options: Lest Israel should credit their idols and graven images

20.template: I have chosen thee in the furnace of {0}

20.question: What did the Lord declare about himself to Jacob and Israel?
options: I am the first, and I am also the last
I am only the God of Jerusalem
I am one among many gods
I dwell only in the temple

20.template: then had thy {0} been as a river

20.question: What did Isaiah command concerning Babylon?
options: Go ye forth of Babylon, flee from the Chaldeans

20.question: What did the Lord say there is for the wicked?

21.title: Isaiah 49 and the isles of the sea
21.text: First Nephi 21 is Isaiah 49. Nephi applied Isaiah's words about scattered Israel and the 'isles of the sea' to his own family, who had crossed the ocean to a far land.

21.question: Whom did the Lord call his servant, in whom he would be glorified?

21.template: give thee for a {0} to the Gentiles

21.question: From what directions and land did Isaiah say Israel would be gathered?
options: From the north, the west, and the land of Sinim
From the south and the east only

21.title: He will not forget Israel
21.text: Isaiah compared the Lord's love to a mother's: even if a woman could forget her nursing child, the Lord would never forget his people - he has graven them upon the palms of his hands.

21.question: What did the Lord say he had done so as not to forget Zion?
options: Graven her upon the palms of his hands
Written her name in the sand

21.question: What did Isaiah say kings and queens would do for Israel?
options: Care for them as nursing fathers and mothers

21.question: By what titles does the Lord close, that all flesh shall know him?
options: Thy Savior and Redeemer, the Mighty One of Jacob

22.question: Did Nephi say Isaiah's words pertained to spiritual or temporal things?
options: To things both temporal and spiritual

22.title: The lost tribes
22.text: Nephi taught that the more part of the tribes of Israel had already been led away and scattered upon the isles of the sea, lost from the knowledge of those at Jerusalem - none knew where they had gone.

22.question: What did Nephi say the Lord would raise up among the Gentiles upon this land?

22.template: all the kindreds of the earth be {0}

22.question: What did Nephi say the Lord would do for scattered Israel?
options: Bring them out of obscurity and darkness, and they shall know their Redeemer
Make them rulers over the Gentiles

22.title: The great whore shall fall
22.text: Nephi declared that the great and abominable church - the whore of all the earth - would war within itself and tumble to the dust; all who fight against Zion shall be destroyed.

22.question: What words of Moses did Nephi cite about a future prophet?
options: A prophet shall the Lord raise up unto you, like unto me
No prophet shall ever come again
The law of Moses shall never change
A king shall rule over Israel forever

22.question: Who did Nephi say was the prophet like Moses of whom Moses spoke?

22.template: there shall be one fold and one {0}

22.title: Satan bound
22.text: Nephi foresaw a day when, because of the righteousness of the Lord's people, Satan would have no power over their hearts and could not be loosed for many years - the millennial reign of the Holy One of Israel.

22.question: What did Nephi say all nations must do to dwell safely in the Holy One of Israel?

pairs.right: Dwell safely in the Holy One of Israel

22.question: How did Nephi say his people would be saved at the last day?
options: By being obedient to the commandments and enduring to the end
By keeping the law of Moses alone
By trusting in Lehi's family only
